evals.prompt: Quero criar um cardápio digital online para restaurantes. A ideia é que o dono do restaurante cadastre os pratos, categorias, preços, e o cliente acesse pelo celular escaneando um QR code. Precisa ter foto dos pratos, opção de marcar favoritos, e filtro por categoria. Minha equipe sou eu e mais um dev junior, ambos com experiência em JavaScript e um pouco de Python. Orçamento apertado, quero começar com um MVP pra validar com uns 10 restaurantes da região. Me ajuda a definir a stack completa?
evals.expected_output: Análise completa com context extraction, classificação como MVP, research de frameworks atuais, landscape com shortlist e discard table, debate entre champions com stacks como Next.js, Remix, ou similar, evaluation de advisors, e síntese final com recomendação primária, alternativas, e caminho de evolução POC→MVP→Full
expectations: Output is entirely in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) except for technical terms
Project is classified as MVP (or POC→MVP path)
At least 3 champions present different stack proposals
A discard table is present with alternatives considered but rejected
The synthesis includes a stage evolution path (POC→MVP→Full)
Specialists are named with their roles and speak in character
Team constraints (2 devs, JS/Python experience) are factored into recommendations
Budget constraints influence the recommendations
Advisor evaluations are produced independently (parallel subagents)
assertions: Output in pt-BR except technical terms
Team constraints (2 devs, JS/Python) factored in

evals.prompt: Estou planejando uma plataforma SaaS B2B de gestão de projetos para agências de marketing digital. Multi-tenant, cada agência tem seus projetos, clientes, tarefas, timeline, e precisa de colaboração em tempo real (tipo quando alguém edita uma tarefa, os outros veem ao vivo). Time de 5 devs, 2 seniors com experiência forte em TypeScript/Node e React, 2 plenos que sabem Python, e 1 junior. Temos 8 meses pra primeira versão. Orçamento de infra de uns R$3000/mês no início. Quero algo que aguente crescer pra 500 agências sem reescrever tudo. Qual stack e arquitetura vocês recomendam?
evals.expected_output: Análise profunda cobrindo multi-tenancy, real-time collaboration, com debate entre stacks considerando a experiência mista do time. Deve incluir discussão de protocolos real-time (WebSocket vs SSE), estratégia de multi-tenancy (schema per tenant vs row-level), e caminho de escala
expectations: Multi-tenancy strategy is explicitly debated (schema-per-tenant vs row-level vs database-per-tenant)
Real-time protocol specialist is invoked as an independent tab-advisor subagent
At least one Vanguard champion presents an emerging alternative
The R$3000/month budget constraint is factored into infrastructure recommendations
Advisors produce independent scores — raw score matrix is visible in synthesis
Cross-examination occurs between champions before advisor subagents are launched
Final synthesis includes consolidated score matrix with divergence analysis
Evolution path from MVP to 500-agency scale is clearly mapped
assertions: Multi-tenancy strategy debated (schema vs row-level vs DB-per-tenant)
At least one Vanguard champion with emerging alternative
R$3000/month budget factored into infra recommendations
Independent advisor scores with raw score matrix
Cross-examination between champions before advisor evaluation
Consolidated score matrix with divergence analysis
Evolution path from MVP to 500-agency scale mapped

evals.prompt: tenho uma ideia meio maluca, quero fazer um framework open source pra criar jogos de RPG de mesa digitais, tipo um toolkit que outros devs usam pra criar sistemas de RPG customizados (tipo D&D, Savage Worlds, etc). precisa ter engine de regras flexível, suporte a fichas de personagem dinâmicas, sistema de rolagem de dados, e uma UI pra jogar online com mapa e tokens. to sozinho por enquanto mas conheço rust e typescript bem. nunca lancei open source antes. por onde começo?
evals.expected_output: Análise reconhecendo a complexidade e escopo ambicioso, classificação como POC com path claro. Debate entre abordagens (monolito vs modular, Rust core + TS frontend, full TS, etc). Discussão sobre rule engine design, open source strategy, e como validar a ideia antes de construir tudo
expectations: Project is classified as POC given solo developer and ambitious scope
The analysis addresses the open-source strategy (licensing, community building)
Rule engine architecture is specifically debated
Both Rust and TypeScript expertise are leveraged in recommendations
A Wildcard or unconventional approach is considered given the ambitious scope
The recommendation includes what to build FIRST to validate the concept
Risk assessment flags solo-developer and scope risks explicitly
assertions: Classified as POC for solo developer with ambitious scope
Recommendation on what to build first for validation
Risk assessment flags solo-developer and scope risks

evals.prompt: Qual formatter devo usar com Python? Estou entre Ruff e Black.
evals.expected_output: Express mode — Scout apresenta shortlist compacta, Moderador dá recomendação direta sem Champions ou Advisors
expectations: Classified as Trivial/Express
No champion presentations
Compact recommendation in ~1 message
Mentions both Ruff and Black with current versions
Output in pt-BR
assertions: Both Ruff and Black mentioned with versions

evals.prompt: Preciso escolher um banco de dados para armazenar dados de IoT — sensores enviando ~100K eventos por segundo. Dados precisam ser consultados por range de tempo. Time de 3 devs com experiência em PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: Padrão mode com debate entre TimescaleDB, InfluxDB, QuestDB e possivelmente ClickHouse. Data-focused advisor invoked with identity card.
expectations: Classified as Médio/Padrão
At least 3 champions for different time-series solutions
A Data-focused advisor is invoked with identity card and random name
PostgreSQL experience is factored into recommendations
Throughput of 100K events/s is addressed in benchmarks
Evolution path from current scale to 10x
assertions: At least 3 champions for time-series solutions
Data-focused advisor invoked with identity card and random name
Evolution path from current to 10x scale

evals.prompt: tenho uma ideia de app, o que vocês acham?
evals.expected_output: Moderador entra em context extraction, fazendo perguntas sobre o que está sendo construído, público-alvo, equipe, etc.
expectations: Context extraction questions are asked before any analysis
At least 3-5 questions from the question bank
No premature recommendations
Moderador persona is present
assertions: Context extraction questions before any analysis
At least 3-5 questions from question bank

evals.prompt: Preciso criar um editor visual drag-and-drop tipo Figma para criar dashboards customizáveis. Time de 3 devs com experiência forte em React. Queremos um MVP em 4 meses.
evals.expected_output: Completo mode com debate sobre canvas libraries (Konva, Fabric.js, react-flow, etc). Frontend and UX advisors invoked with identity cards.
expectations: Classified as Alto/Completo
A Frontend-focused advisor is invoked with identity card and random name
A UX-focused advisor is invoked with identity card and random name
Canvas/rendering library debate is central
MVP timeline of 4 months factors into recommendations
At least one Vanguard champion
assertions: Frontend-focused advisor invoked with identity card and random name
UX-focused advisor invoked with identity card and random name

evals.prompt: Preciso migrar nossa aplicação de Heroku para algo mais barato. Temos 3 microserviços em Node.js, banco PostgreSQL no RDS, Redis para cache. Cerca de 50K usuários mensais. Orçamento máximo de R$500/mês para infra.
evals.expected_output: Padrão mode com debate entre Railway, Fly.io, DigitalOcean, e possivelmente Hetzner/Coolify. Infrastructure advisors invoked with identity cards.
expectations: Infrastructure-focused advisors are invoked with identity cards and random names
Cost projection is central to recommendations
R$500/month budget constraint is respected
Migration path from Heroku is explicitly addressed
Existing stack (Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis) is preserved or justified if changed
assertions: Infrastructure-focused advisors invoked with identity cards and random names
Existing stack (Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis) preserved or justified

evals.prompt: Estou entre Next.js e Remix para o frontend do meu projeto. Já tenho o backend pronto em Node. Time de 2 devs com experiência em React. Me ajuda a decidir?
evals.expected_output: Rápido mode com 2 Champions (Next.js e Remix), matriz simplificada, recomendação direta
expectations: Classified as Simples/Rápido
Exactly 2 champions (one for each option)
Simplified comparison matrix present
No cross-examination (Rápido mode)
Backend compatibility is addressed

evals.prompt: Qual ORM usar com PostgreSQL em Node.js? Quero que façam a análise no modo completo.
evals.expected_output: Apesar de ser uma decisão simples, o modo completo é usado conforme pedido do usuário. Champions para Prisma, Drizzle, TypeORM/Knex, etc.
expectations: User override to Completo mode is respected
At least 3 champions present full proposals
Cross-examination phase occurs
Full comparative matrix with weights
ORM-specific criteria (migrations, type safety, query builder vs schema-first)
assertions: User override to Completo mode respected
At least 3 champions with full proposals
ORM-specific criteria covered (migrations, type safety, query builder)

evals.prompt: /tab "analise esse projeto, tá uma bagunça e preciso saber por onde começar a arrumar"
evals.expected_output: Analyze/Improve mode — detects messy Node.js project with outdated deps, SQL injection, hardcoded credentials, mixed databases, deprecated packages. Prioritized improvement recommendations.
expectations: Intent classified as Analyze or Improve
SQL injection vulnerability in /users endpoint identified
Hardcoded database credentials flagged
Deprecated packages identified (request, moment)
Mixed database usage (MySQL + MongoDB) questioned
Security vulnerabilities prioritized over code quality issues
Improvement roadmap with priority order provided
assertions: Security issues prioritized over code quality

evals.prompt: /tab "quero migrar esse projeto para algo mais moderno e seguro"
evals.expected_output: Improve mode — detects messy project, Champions propose modernization strategies (not just 'use React'). Focus on security fixes, dependency updates, architecture cleanup.
expectations: Intent classified as Improve
Current stack auto-detected (Express, mongoose, mysql, ejs)
Champions propose improvement strategies, not full rewrites
Security fixes (SQL injection, credentials) are top priority
Dependency modernization path (request->fetch/axios, moment->date-fns/dayjs)
Database consolidation strategy proposed
Output in pt-BR
assertions: Current stack detected: Express, mongoose, mysql, ejs
Champions propose improvements, not full rewrites
